summary: node-fsagent ships a covert exfiltration payload targeting Codex CLI credentials
details: The package ships archive-sender.js, a self-contained exfiltration payload that tars and gzips /root/.codex (the OpenAI Codex CLI per-user credential/config directory), splits the archive into ~50KB chunks, and PUTs each chunk to https://registry.npmjs.org as new versions of the 'node-fsagent' package, embedding the chunk bytes in the _attachments field. Authorization uses a hardcoded npm bearer token that is stored as two integer arrays and reconstructed at runtime via a String.fromCharCode XOR loop (decoding to an 'npm_'-prefixed token), abusing the npm registry itself as a covert channel to an attacker-controlled account. package.json declares no scripts, main, or bin entries, so the payload does not auto-execute on npm install or on require(); it is designed to be dropped and invoked directly (e.g. `node archive-sender.js`) as staging infrastructure after a separate initial foothold. The combination of a well-known credential-directory read, XOR-obfuscated hardcoded credential, and covert registry-publish exfiltration channel is unambiguously malicious tooling.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
